summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (Issue #711) so users on homeservers using Matrix Authentication Service (MAS/MSC3861) can connect to Moltis, which previously only supported password and access_token auth modes.
summary.outcome: Full OIDC two-phase browser flow implemented across Rust backend and JS frontend, PR #730 created and merged, with multiple post-implementation bug fixes addressing MAS registration errors, wrong callback path, status detection, UI display bugs, ownership mode passthrough, and a Safari button repaint issue.

repo: matrix-sdk 0.16 OIDC API uses client.oauth() — no feature flags required; handles PKCE, dynamic client registration, token exchange, and auto token refresh
MAS (matrix.org's auth service) rejects https://localhost redirect URIs — must normalize to http://localhost per RFC 8252 loopback rules
MAS rejects localhost as client_uri — client_uri must be a real HTTPS project URL (e.g. https://moltis.org), not the server address
The OAuth callback route is /auth/callback, not /api/oauth/callback
matrix_auth().logged_in() returns false for OIDC sessions — must use client.user_id().is_some() to detect any active session
ChannelPlugin trait methods must be implemented in impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin block, not as inherent methods — the gateway dispatches through dyn ChannelPlugin
Channel status uses ch.status === 'connected' field, not ch.connected
The ownership card in page-channels.js checks auth mode against only 'password' — OIDC fell through to the 'access token' message
The gateway's oauth_start built a hardcoded minimal config, silently dropping ownership_mode, dm_policy, room_policy from the form
oidc_issuer field was documented but had no consumption path in matrix-sdk 0.16 — removed to avoid dead config

code.finding: was: state.client.matrix_auth().logged_in() — returns false for OIDC sessions; fixed to state.client.user_id().is_some()
normalize_redirect_uri() rewrites https://localhost:* to http://localhost:* for MAS compatibility, but client_uri must remain a real HTTPS project URL
oidc_start/oidc_complete added as default trait methods so gateway can dispatch through dyn ChannelPlugin
oauth_start built hardcoded config with only homeserver/auth_mode/access_token, losing all other form fields — fixed to merge full config from params
ownership card logic only handled 'password' — anything else showed 'Access token auth is always user-managed'
provider-btn had transition: opacity 0.15s + opacity: 0.4 on :disabled — caused Safari to composite old text pixels during transition, creating ghost text overlay

workflow: Safari does not repaint text nodes inside <button> elements reliably when Preact patches them — fix is to key the button element on its state so Preact destroys and recreates the DOM node
Greptile automated code review caught real bugs: wrong response field (res.result vs res.payload), missing file permissions (0o600 for session files), and dead config fields
Running cargo check / clippy on only modified crates is faster than workspace-wide checks during iterative development
matrix-sdk types like RoomLoadSettings are re-exported from matrix_sdk::store — no need to add matrix-sdk-base as direct dependency
Preact/HTM does not need nested ternaries extracted to variables for correctness, but keying components on state changes is necessary for reliable DOM updates in Safari

friction: Multiple round-trips to fix MAS registration errors without being able to test against the live MAS server — led to: first fixing client_uri, then wrong redirect scheme, then wrong callback path (/api/oauth/callback vs /auth/callback)
The redirect_uri normalization (https -> http) was removed then had to be restored, causing a regression that required another user test cycle

Safari ghost button text issue took 7+ attempts to fix: tried ternary extraction, key on phase, position/isolation CSS, opacity->color-mix CSS, span wrapper, keying span, and finally keying the button element itself
oidc_start/oidc_complete implemented as inherent MatrixPlugin methods instead of trait overrides — silently fell through to default 'not supported' error requiring a test cycle to catch
The gateway's oauth_start silently dropped ownership_mode from the form, discovered only after the user tested and saw 'User-managed' despite checking the moltis-owned box
Greptile ran 4 review iterations with stale comments appearing on new commits, requiring manual resolution of already-fixed comments each round
open_items: E2E tests for OIDC are UI-only mocks — no integration test against a real MAS server; would catch redirect URI, client_uri, and callback path issues automatically
The 're-authenticate' button for existing OIDC channels (EditMatrixModal) was mentioned in the plan but its implementation was not confirmed working
Duplicate Matrix account detection was added but only for same user_id — connecting same homeserver with different accounts is still possible
token refresh persistence background task (spawn_session_persistence_task) spawned but not tested — unclear if tokens are persisted correctly across restarts
bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed for OIDC moltis_owned mode was referenced in the plan but not confirmed working in the session
The normalize_redirect_uri function only handles localhost — Tailscale or other non-loopback HTTPS URLs used as redirect targets are not addressed
Re-authentication flow for expired OIDC sessions needs testing — the restore_oidc_session path on startup after token expiry has not been exercised
